assertions.assertion: Identifies Data Protection key ring as the root cause — each instance generates its own keys
assertions.rationale: Clearly states 'each BFF instance generates its own ASP.NET Core Data Protection key ring by default' and explains the cookie decryption failure

assertions.assertion: Recommends configuring a shared key store (e.g., PersistKeysToDbContext, PersistKeysToAzureBlobStorage, or Redis)
assertions.rationale: Shows three options: PersistKeysToDbContext, PersistKeysToAzureBlobStorage, and PersistKeysToStackExchangeRedis

assertions.assertion: Recommends server-side sessions with a shared store (AddEntityFrameworkServerSideSessions) instead of in-memory sessions
assertions.rationale: Shows AddEntityFrameworkServerSideSessions with UseSqlServer and warns against AddServerSideSessions without EF

assertions.assertion: Shows code for AddDataProtection() with a persistent key store
assertions.rationale: Shows builder.Services.AddDataProtection().PersistKeysToDbContext<ApplicationDbContext>() and two other options with full code
